crime.value: Kidnapping and attempted murder by burial alive in an oversized coffin
crime.quote: They rushed in and overpowered her with their chloroform, carried her down, poured more into the coffin

victim.value: Lady Frances Carfax, targeted for her valuable jewelry and wealth
victim.quote: He has begun to pawn the jewels. We should get him now.

closed_world.value: London pawnshop network and criminal underworld where the perpetrators operate
closed_world.quote: It is possible that he will go to some other pawnbroker in the future

culprit_and_motive.value: The Shlessingers (ex-missionary couple) motivated by theft of Lady Frances's jewelry
culprit_and_motive.quote: If our ex-missionary friends escape the clutches of Lestrade

resolution.value: Holmes deduces the oversized coffin was meant for two bodies and stops the burial in time
resolution.quote: Why so large a coffin for so small a body? To leave room for another body.

investigator.value: Sherlock Holmes as the primary detective with Watson as narrator
investigator.quote: But why Turkish?' asked Mr. Sherlock Holmes

suspect_architecture.value: Primary suspects are the Shlessingers (ex-missionary couple) - husband who pawns jewelry and wife who delivers items. Secondary figure is the undertaker complicit in coffin scheme. Clear means (chloroform, oversized coffin), motive (theft), opportunity (isolated victim)
suspect_architecture.quote: It was the wife, this time, but the pendant she brought was the fellow of the other. She is a tall, pale woman

red_herring.value: The focus on conventional murder methods and locations while the true scheme involves live burial in an oversized coffin under false death certificate
red_herring.quote: Why so large a coffin for so small a body? To leave room for another body. Both would be buried under the one certificate

clue_fairness.value: The undertaker's wife's remark about the coffin being 'out of the ordinary' and taking longer is presented to reader through Philip Green's report before Holmes interprets its significance
clue_fairness.quote: It should be there before now. It took longer, being out of the ordinary

social_world.value: Upper class vulnerability (Lady Frances), military connections (Admiral Green's son Philip), professional networks (pawnbrokers, undertakers), and ex-colonial missionary background of perpetrators
social_world.quote: the son of the famous admiral of that name who commanded the Sea of Azof fleet in the Crimean War

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical demonstration through the oversized coffin dimensions and the undertaker's timing comment, plus the pattern of jewelry pawning that leads to tracking the perpetrators
reveal_mechanic.quote: Then in an instant I remembered the deep sides, and the little wasted figure at the bottom

investigator_wound.value: Holmes experiences self-doubt about missing obvious clues, haunted by the thought that he dismissed important observations too easily
investigator_wound.quote: My night was haunted by the thought that somewhere a clue, a strange sentence, a curious observation, had come

_meta.title: ebook sir arthur conan doyle the disappearance of lady frances carfax
